content:
# Storativity

**Summary**: The volume of water released from an aquifer per unit decline in head.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1993-moallim|Moallim, 1993]] calculated storage coefficients to determine the amount of water available for extraction.

## Scientific Context

- [[1993-moallim|Moallim, 1993]] Determines the capacity of the aquifer to sustain drawdown over time.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[1993-moallim|Moallim, 1993]] Calculated using Jacob's method or derived from specific yield and porosity.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The storage coefficient of the Baidoa aquifer was found to be 7.50 x 10^-3. ([[1993-moallim|Moallim, 1993, p. 93]])
